expectations.evidence: ReviewCard.tsx exists in the outputs directory (3089 bytes) with a fully implemented component including props interface, avatar, rating, body, and date sections.

The component was created as a flat file `components/ReviewCard.tsx`, not as `ReviewCard/index.tsx`. The transcript confirms: 'Discovered components/ReviewCard.tsx already existed'. The import in page.tsx is `import ReviewCard from "@/components/ReviewCard"` which points to a flat file, not a folder with index.tsx.
The output file is `ReviewCard.tsx` (flat file). The transcript says the executor updated `components/ReviewCard.tsx` directly. No folder structure was created.
Component is named `ReviewCard` (PascalCase) — visible in `export default function ReviewCard` at line 14 of the output file.
Lines 36-49: A div with `data-testid="review-card-avatar"` renders either an `` (when avatarUrl provided) or an initials-based fallback div.
Lines 59-79: A div with `data-testid="review-card-rating"` and `aria-label` renders 5 SVG star icons, filled amber for rated stars and zinc for empty ones.
Tailwind classes used extensively throughout: `flex`, `flex-col`, `gap-3`, `rounded-xl`, `border`, `border-zinc-200`, `bg-white`, `p-5`, `dark:border-zinc-800`, `text-sm`, `text-amber-400`, etc.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Searched the full ReviewCard.tsx output: none of useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, or useMutation appear anywhere in the file. The component is purely presentational.
No folder was created. The component is a flat file `components/ReviewCard.tsx`, so there is no camelCase folder `reviewCard/` at all.

The component is placed at `components/ReviewCard.tsx` — directly under `components/`, not under `components/common/` or any domain subfolder. The transcript confirms this path. However, looking at the existing components (ProductCard.tsx, StatsCard.tsx, EmptyState.tsx), they all appear to be flat files directly under `components/`, which means the project convention itself may place components at `components/` root. The assertion specifically requires `common/` or `{domain}/` nesting, which is not present.

claims.claim: TypeCheck passed with zero ReviewCard-related errors
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 6: 'Ran pnpm exec tsc --noEmit — confirmed zero errors related to ReviewCard or showcase page.'

claims.claim: ESLint passed clean on both files
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 7: 'Ran pnpm exec eslint components/ReviewCard.tsx app/showcase/page.tsx — clean, no errors or warnings.'

claims.claim: Avatar falls back to initials when no avatarUrl provided
claims.evidence: Lines 21-26 compute initials from authorName; lines 44-47 render them in a styled div when avatarUrl is falsy.

claims.claim: Matched border-radius convention from other components (rounded-xl)
claims.evidence: Line 30 uses `rounded-xl`. Transcript notes this matches ProductCard/StatsCard convention.
Multiple dark: variants present: dark:border-zinc-800, dark:bg-zinc-900, dark:text-zinc-50, dark:text-zinc-300, dark:text-zinc-500, dark:text-zinc-600, dark:bg-zinc-700.

suggestions.reason: The existing fixture project places all components (ProductCard, StatsCard, EmptyState, Button) as flat files directly under components/. This assertion may be testing a convention the project doesn't actually follow. Consider verifying what the project's real convention is before asserting subdirectory placement — otherwise the skill is penalized for correctly following existing patterns.

Similarly, the existing components in the fixture are all flat files (ProductCard.tsx, StatsCard.tsx, etc.), not folder/index.tsx. The skill followed the existing codebase convention. If the intent is to test that the skill follows a *specified* convention rather than the *observed* convention, the eval prompt should explicitly state the folder/index.tsx requirement.

No assertion checks whether the component exports correctly (default export) or whether 'use client' directive is present — both are conventions visible in the existing components and present in the output.

eval_feedback.overall: Four assertions fail, but three of them (folder structure, camelCase folder, domain placement) test conventions that conflict with the actual fixture project's patterns. The skill correctly mimicked existing component conventions. Consider aligning assertions with the fixture's real structure, or making the eval prompt explicitly request the folder-based pattern.
